thismodre: This module implements real-time messaging between users for an application.

youcanvisit: You can visit Chat module package list page to see list of packages related with this module.
thisisthe: This is the page that users send messages to each other.
messt: Message (aggregate root): Represents a chat message. Implements IMultiTenant.
chatuse: ChatUser (aggregate root): Represents a chat user.
receivermsg: UserMessage: is created for each side (sender and receiver) of a message.
stattes: Side: States that if it is a send message or received message.
conver: Conversation: is created for each side of a conversation between users. Implements IMultiTenant and IAggregateRoot interfaces.
targetus: TargetUserId: Id of other related ChatUser.
lastmsgside: LastMessageSide: States the side of the latest message (send or received).
lastmsg: LastMessage: Content of the last message in the conversation.
lastmsgdate: LastMessageDate: Date of the last message in the conversation.
repo: UnreadMessageCount: Count of unread messages in the conversation.

conversationappservice: ConversationAppService (implements IConversationAppService): Used to send messages, get conversation between users and mark a conversation as read.
conatactappser: ContactAppService (implements IContactAppService): Used to get list of contacts and total unread message count of a user.
distributedevent: DistributedEventBusRealTimeChatMessageSender(implements IRealTimeChatMessageSender): Used to publish chat messages to distributed event bus.
signalR: SignalRRealTimeChatMessageSender(implements IRealTimeChatMessageSender): Used to send messages to target SignalR client.
thismodulecap: This module extends the open-source CMS Kit module and adds additional CMS (Content Management System) capabilities to your application.
follfeature: The following features are provided by the open source CMS Kit module:
blogg: system to create publish blog posts with multiple blog support.
systemtotag: system to tag any kind of resource, like a blog post.
systemtoadd: system to add comments feature to any kind of resource, like blog post or a product review page.
systemreact: system to add reactions (smileys) feature to any kind of resource, like a blog post or a comment.
ratingfeature: system to add rating feature to any kind of resource.
managepub: system to manage public menus dynamically
globalstyle: system to add global styles and scripts dynamically.
createsynamic: system to create dynamic widgets for page and blog posts.
andthe: And the following features are provided by the CMS Kit pro version:
systemtoallow: system to allow users to subscribe to newsletters.
towrite: system to allow users to write messages to you.
createurl: system to create URLs that redirect to other pages or external websites.
quickoll: system to create quick polls for users
onafeat: Click on a feature to understand and learn how to use it. See the module description page for an overview of the module features.
ifwant: If you want to add the CMS kit to your existing solution, you can use the ABP CLI add-module command:
opentheglobal: Open the GlobalFeatureConfigurator class in the Domain.Shared project and place the following code to the Configure method to enable all open-source and commercial features in the CMS Kit module.
alternative: Alternatively, you can enable features individually, like cmsKit.Comments.Enable();.
ifyour: If you are using Entity Framework Core, do not forget to add a new migration and update your database.

usingThe: Using the ABP CLI to create a new project
useThe: Use the new command of the ABP CLI to create a new project:
tArgument: -t argument specifies the startup template name. app-pro is the startup template that contains the essential ABP Commercial Modules pre-installed and configured for you.
weCan: We can also use a different level of namespaces; e.g. Bookstore, Acme.Bookstore or Acme.Retail.BookStore.
aNEw: A new, modern and stylish raaghu theme with different color options, it’s the newest theme. You can choose one out of 3 options Dark, Light and Semi Dark.
abpCliDoc: ABP CLI document covers all of the available commands and options for the ABP CLI. The main difference for the ABP Commercial is the template names. See theABP Commercial Startup Templates document for other commercial templates.
theSolHas: The solution has a layered structure (based on the Domain Driven Design) and contains unit & integration test projects. See the solution structure document to understand the solution structure in details.
suiteto: Use ABP Suite to create a new project
abpccl: Use ABP CLI to create a new project
csva: Creating a new ABP Commercial solution
tar: There are 2 ways of creating a new ABP Commercial solution. You can use the ABP CLI or ABP Suite.
iyhn: If you have not yet installed ABP CLI, you need to install it with the following command. To check if you already have it, write abp to the command line.
tcys: To create your solution, go to the directory where you want to create in and type the following command:
acme: Acme.BookStore is the project name. Your Visual Studio Solution, .sln file will be named as Acme.BookStore.sln. The projects inside the root folder and all the namespaces will have the prefix Acme.BookStore.*. You can give different project names like onlyBookStore or Acme.Retail.BookStore. You can use single level, two-level or three-level naming.
tot: -t or --template is the template name. For commercial apps, use app-pro.
specify: To discover other CLI new project options type:
ttp: The template provides multiple UI frameworks:
mvc: mvc: ASP.NET Core MVC UI with Razor Pages (default)
isui: Use -u or --ui option to specify the UI framework:
ttst: The template supports the following database providers:
databasepro: Use -d or --database-provider option to specify the database provider:
asay: ABP Suite allows you to create a new ABP solution.
iyh: If you have not installed ABP Suite, see how to install ABP Suite.
cccc: Creating solution via ABP CLI
solute: Creating solution via ABP Suite

abpc: ABP Commercial modules are designed to be extensible and customizable. They fully implement the extensibility system that is defined by the ABP Framework. See the Customizing the Application Modules guide to learn different ways of extending/customizing a module.
thismduse: This module is used to upload, download and organize files in a hierarchical folder structure. It is also compatible to multi-tenancy and you can determine total size limit for your tenants.
reactblob: This module is based on the BLOB Storing system, so it can use different storage providers to store the file contents.
seedes: See the module description page for an overview of the module features.
baseonthe: File Management module is based on the BLOB Storing system as defined before, and it uses FileManagementContainer as a BLOB container.
filemgtmod: File Management module is based on the BLOB Storing system as defined before, and it uses FileManagementContainer as a BLOB container.
mustset: You must set a BLOB provider for FileManagementContainer.
pac: Please check the BLOB Storage Providers documentation for more information about providers and how to use them.

folders: File Management page is used to create folders, upload files and view the list of folders and files that stored in the application.
byclick: You can create a new folder by clicking Create Folder button that located at top right on the page. The folder will be created at active directory.
canmove: You can move a folder to another directory on the left tree view.
folderby: You can rename a folder by clicking Actions -> Rename on the table.
willopen: You can upload files by clicking Upload Files button that located at top right on the page. This will open a new modal for selecting your local files to upload. The files will be uploaded at active directory.
movefiles: You can move files by clicking Actions -> Move on the data table.
dataSeed: You can rename a file by clicking Actions -> Rename on the data table.

isuse: DirectoryManager is used to manage your folders like create, rename, move and delete.

allowsyouto: This module allows you to create questionnaires to gather information. The forms module can store responses as they come in and you can export the data to a CSV file. You can share your form with others with your form unique link. You can request authentication or allow anonymous reply. It is similar to the Google Form application. Usage area is quite wide, you can create surveys, manage event registrations, collect email addresses for a newsletter, create a quiz, and even receive an order request.
internals: See the module description page for an overview of the module features.
thismodfol: This module follows the Entity Best Practices & Conventions guide.
rootofthe: The main aggregate root of the form entities. The form options, title and description is being stored on this entity.
storesque: It stores questions of the form. This entity is dependent to form entity by <span class='txt-color'>FormId.
formsub: Each form submit is a new form response record. The form response has answer records.
modfollthe: This module follows the Repository Best Practices & Conventions guide.
csutomrepo: Following custom repositories are defined for this module:

frmform: FrmFormResponses: A new form response is being created each time user submits the form.
formper: See the FormsPermissions class members for all permissions defined for this module.
thisTut: This tutorial assumes that you've already purchased an ABP Commercial license and have an active ABP Commercial account.
thisDoc: This document assumes that you refer to use raaghu-react Pages as the UIframework and Entity Framework Core as the database provider.
tutExplain: This tutorial explains how to create and run a new web application using the application startup template.
isc: IdentityServer configuration may be different based on deployment configurations. Basically, you need update identityserver client related data and update your hosting preferences based on your deployment environment.
coo: Cors origins configuration for gateways, microservices swagger authorization and React must be updated for deployment. This can be found under App configuration in appsettings.json
tcm: This configuration must be done if React used as back-office web application. It is found under App configuration in appsettings.json
thismoduse: This module implements the User and Role system of an application;
managerol: Manage roles and users in the system. A user is allowed to have multiple roles.
roleanduser: Set permissions in role and user levels.
enadisa: Enable/disable two factor authentication and user lockout per user.
inthesys: Manage claim types in the system, set claims to roles and users.
settpage: Setting page to manage password complexity, user sign-in, account and lockout.
sociallog: Supports social login integrations (Twitter, Facebook, GitHub etc...).
packages: View security logs (login, logout, change password...) in the system.
moddes: See the module description page for an overview of the module features.
thsmodf: This module follows the module development best practices guide and consists of several NuGet and NPM packages. See the guide if you want to understand the packages and relations between them.
toseelist: You can visit Identity module package list page to see list of packages related with this module.
addthefoll: Identity module adds the following items to the "Main" menu, under the "Administration" menu item:
classhas: IdentityMenuNames class has the constants for the menu item names.
useto: Roles page is used to manage roles in the system. A role is a set of permissions assigned to the users.
cancreate: You can create a new role or edit a role in this page:
rp: Default roles are assigned to new users by default.
pubrol: Public roles are visible to other users.
manca: You can manage permissions of a role:
perisa: A permission is an action of the application granted to roles and users.
witha: A user with a role will inherit all the permissions granted for the role.
modcan: Any module can define permissions. Once you define a new permission, it will be available in this page.
leftside: Left side is the list of modules. Once you click to a module name, you can check/uncheck permissions related to that module.
setcust: You can set custom claim values for a role:
um: Claim types are retrieved from the claim list defined in the Claim Types Management page (see below).
isuseds: Users page is used to manage the users in your system.
cancre: You can create a new user or edit an existing user in this page:
canhav: A user can have zero or more roles in the system.
twofact: You can set two factor verification and user lockout settings per user.
unionof: A user has union of the permissions of the assigned roles. Identity module also allows to grant extra permissions to a specific user.
isan: A permission is an action of the application granted to roles and users.
withrole: A user with a role will inherit all the permissions granted for the role.
ctm: Any module can define permissions. Once you define a new permission, it will be available in this page.
sideis: Left side is the list of modules. Once you click to a module name, you can check/uncheck permissions related to that module.
customclaim: Identity module allows to define custom claim types.
usedtostore: Custom claims can be used to store additional information to a user or role.
OU: Custom claim values then can be accessed in the application code for an authenticated user.
arealso: Claim Types are also used by the OpenIddict module if you're using it.
isusedto: Organization units page is used to manage organization units, members of organization units and roles of organization units.
unitoredit: You can add a new organization unit or edit an existing organization unit on this page. In order to add a new root organization unit, click "New root unit" button and fill the opening form.
unitto: In order to add a sub-unit to an existing organization unit, right click on an existing organization unit and click "Add sub-unit" context menu item. Similarly, in order to edit an organization unit, right click on an existing organization unit and click "Edit" context menu item.
youcanmange: You can manage members of an organization unit using the members tab by selection an organization unit
securitypage: Security logs page is used to search and view authentication related operations (login, logout, change password...) in the system.
thismodimpd: This module implements the Language management system of an application;
descpage: See the module description page for an overview of the module features.
addthefo: Language management module adds the following items to the "Main" menu, under the "Administration" menu item:
pagescon: LanguageManagementMenuNames class has the constants for the menu item names.
managelang: Languages page is used to manage languages in the system.
newedit: You can create a new language or edit an existing language in this page:
enab: Enabled languages can be selected as the system language.
pageisused: Language texts page is used to manage texts in different languages.
textfora: You can translate a text for a language or edit the already existing translation in this page.
modprov: This module provides integration and management functionality for the OpenIddict library;
manageapp: Manage Application and API scopes in the system.
foran: See the module description page for an overview of the module features.
addthe: The OpenIddict module adds the following items to the "Main" menu, under the "Administration" menu item:
openidd: OpenIddictProMenus class has the constants for the menu item names.
isuso: Applications page is used to manage OpenIddict applications. An application represent hosted applications that can request tokens from your authentication server.
oreditexit: You can create new application or edit existing applications in this page:
manageapi: OpenIddict module allows to manage API scope. To allow applications to request access tokens for APIs, you need to define API scopes.
newapi: You can create a new API resource or edit an existing API resource in this page:
abprovides: ABP Commercial provides production ready, enterprise level application modules. See the modules page for a list and descriptions of the modules as an overview. This documentation is for developers and explains technical details of the modules.
acli: Account: Login, register, forgot password, email activation, social logins and other account related functionalities.
logg: Audit logging: Reporting the user audit logs and entity histories in details.
realtime: Chat: Real time messaging between users of the application.
asetof: CMS Kit: A set of reusable CMS (Content Management System) building blocks.
uplo: File Management: Upload, download and organize files in a hierarchical folder structure.
permngt: Identity: User, role, claims and permission management.
openid: OpenIddict: Managing the openiddict objects like applications, scopes.
managingtheid: Identity Server: Managing the identity server objects like clients, API resources, identity resources, secrets, application URLs, claims and more.
addorremove: Language management: Add or remove languages and localize the application UI on the fly.
mngtenant: SaaS: Manage tenants, editions and features to create your multi-tenant / SaaS application.
managetext: Text template management: Manage text templates in the system.
paygateway: Payment module implements payment gateway integration of an application. It provides one time payment and recurring payment options.
overviewof: See the module description page for an overview of the module features.
useapay: In order to use a Payment Gateway, you need to add related NuGet packages to your related project as explained in Manual Installation section above and add DependsOn to your related module. For example, if you don't want to use PayU, you don't have to use its NuGet packages.
tourapp: After adding packages of a payment gateway to your application, you also need to configure global payment module options and options for the payment modules you have added. See the Options section below.
ifyourequire: If you require a different payment gateway than existing ones, you can create a custom payment gateway by your own. 2 steps are required to create a custom payment gateway. First is creating a payment gateway object that implements IPaymentGateway. This interface exposes core payment operations without any UI. Second step is creating UI for the payment gateway. This UI is used to redirect user to payment gateway and validate payment.
createcustomgate: Follow the instructions here to create a custom payment gateway.
uitt: This module follows the module development best practices guide and consists of several NuGet and NPM packages. See the guide if you want to understand the packages and relations between them.
canvisitpay: You can visit Payment module package list page to see list of packages related with this module
thisallow: This page allows selecting a payment gateway. If there is one payment gateway configured for final application, this page will be skipped.
sendname: This page is used to send Name, Surname and Email Address of user to PayU.
payplansforsub: Payment plans for subscriptions can be managed on this page. You can connect external subscriptions for each gateway to a plan.
seccreate: Creating a custom payment gateway
check: Check the connection string in the appsettings.json file under the .Web project.
about: About the Connection Strings and Database Management Systems
theSolEF: The solution is configured to use Entity Framework Core with MS SQL Server by default. However, if you've selected another DBMS using the -dbms parameter on the ABP CLI new command (like -dbms MySQL), the connection string might be different for you.
EFC: EF Core supports various database providers and you can use any supported DBMS. See the Entity Framework integration document to learn how to switch to another DBMS if you need later.
TheSolS: The solution uses the Entity Framework Core Code First Migrations. It comes with a .DbMigrator console application which applies the migrations and also seeds the initial data. It is useful on development as well as on production environment.
db: .DbMigrator project has its own appsettings.json. So, if you have changed the connection string above, you should also change this one.
DBapp: .DbMigrator application automatically creates the Initial migration on first run.
ifYouvs: If you are using Visual Studio, you can skip to the Running the DbMigrator section. However, other IDEs (e.g. Rider) may have problems for the first run since it adds the initial migration and compiles the project. In this case, open a command line terminal in the folder of the .DbMigrator project and run the following command:
RTC: Right click to the .DbMigrator project and select Set as StartUp Project
Hit: Hit F5 (or Ctrl+F5) to run the application. It will have an output like shown below:
run: Initial seed data creates the admin user in the database (with the password is .1q2w3E*) which is then used to login to the application. So, you need to use .DbMigrator at least once for a new database.
Warn: Warning: When you create an ABP solution, the client-side packages are being restored by ABP CLI and Suite. But if you fetch the source-code that's commited by another team member, your libs folder will be empty. Before starting the application, run abp install-libs command in your Web directory to restore the client-side libraries. This will populate the libs folder. Ensure that the .Web project is the startup project. Run the application which will open the login page in your browser:
USe: Use Ctrl+F5 in Visual Studio (instead of F5) to run the application without debugging. If you don't have a debug purpose, this will be faster.
TheApp: The application is up and running. You can start developing your application based on this startup template.
thismodmanage: This module is used to manage your tenants and editions in multi-tenant applications;
tenantsand: Manage tenants and editions in the system. A tenant is allowed to have one edition.
overviewoffea: See the module description page for an overview of the module features.
thismodfollrel: This module follows the module development best practices guide and consists of several NuGet and NPM packages. See the guide if you want to understand the packages and relations between them.
packagesrelated: You can visit SaaS module package list page to see list of packages related with this module.
toeditions: SaaS module implements subscribing to Editions for Tenants using Payment module. To enable it, project must contain Volo.Saas and Volo.Payment modules and these modules must be configured as shown below.
paymodd: Firstly, Payment module must be configured properly:
oryou: Or you can install via using ABP Suite.
ccy: Configure Saas module to use Payment.
confsaa: Configure Saas module to use Payment.
follthe: Follow the subscriptions section of Payment Module Documentation. Complete enabling webhooks and configuring plans sections.
runtheapp: Run the application and go to Saas > Editions page at your Web Application menu.
usage: Create or Edit an existing Edition. Plan dropdown must be visible if you've done earlier steps correctly. Pick a Plan for Edition.
containeapub: SaaS module doesn't contain a public facing list page for listing editions for new customers/tenants to subscribe. First, you need to create such a page in your application. Then, when a new customer/tenant selects one of those Editions, you can create a subscription and redirect user to payment module as shown below.
inje: Inject ISubscriptionAppService to create a subscription for a edition:
whenthepay: When the payment is completed successfully, the tenant and edition relation will be updated according to subscription status. Make sure Payment Gateway Web Hooks are configured properly.
uiafter: After all, payment module will redirect user to the callbackUrl if configured in payment configuration with a paymentRequestId parameter. In this page, you can check the status of the payment request and show a success message to the user when the payment status is confirmed. Since the payment confirmation is asynchronous, you need to check the payment status repeatedly until it is confirmed.
follite: SaaS module adds the following items to the "Main" menu, under the "Administration" menu item:
saashost: SaasHostMenuNames and SaasTenantMenuNames classes have the constants for the menu item names.
isusee: Tenant page is used to manage tenants in the system.
cs: You can create a new tenant or edit a tenant in this page:
managecon: You can manage connection string of a tenant in case you want to use a separate database for a specific tenant. If you want to use Host database for a tenant, select "Use the Shared Database" option.
TF: You can also use the module-specific database connection string feature. In this case, you should select the "Use module specific database connection string" option, then you can determine your modules and their connection strings. Before adding you can check your connection by clicking "Check"
youcansette: You can set features of tenants.
editpageuse: Editions page is used to manage the editions in your system.
EF: You can create a new edition or edit an existing edition in this page:
anedit: You can set features of an edition in this page:
thisTutabp: This tutorial assumes that you’ve already purchased an ABP Commercial license and have an active ABP Commercial account.
docAssume: This document assumes that you refer to use raaghu-react Pages as the UI framework and Entity Framework Core as the database provider.
letSet: Let’s setup your development environment before creating the first project.
theFollowing: The following tools should be installed on your development machine.
vss: Visual Studio 2022 (v17.3+) for Windows / Visual Studio for Mac. 1
npm: npm v8 and above or Yarn v1.20+ (not v2) 2.
abpCli: ABP CLI is a command line interface that is used to automate some common tasks for ABP based solutions.
abp: ABP CLI is a free & open source tool for the ABP framework. It is also used for ABP Commercial application development.
first: First, you need to install the ABP CLI using the following command:
ifYou: If you've already installed, you can update it using the following command:
inOrderCli: In order to use ABP Commercial features, you need to login your account using the ABP CLI:
itWill: It will ask a password, so you must enter the password of your account.
youCan: You can freely create a new account from the ABP Account web site. However, your account should be registered to an organization as a developer to be able to use the ABP Commercial. If your company has an ABP Commercial license, ask your manager to add your account to the developer list of the organization.
aSuite: ABP Suite is an application aims to assist you on your development.
ywg: You will get a slightly different solution structure, based on the options you have specified.
iyd: If you don't specify any additional option, you will have a solution in the aspnet-core folder like the below:
proare: Projects are located in aspnet-core/src and aspnet-core/test folders. While the aspnet-core/src folder contains the actual application, aspnet-core/test folder contains unit tests and test base projects. The below diagram shows the layers & project dependencies of the application:
ES: Each section below describes the related project and its dependencies.
tpc: This project contains constants, enums and the other objects which are part of the domain layer, but shared across by all projects in the solution.
fex: For example BookType or BookConsts (contains validation constants like MaxNameLength) are good candidates to be in the *.Domain.Shared project.
domains: Domain.Shared project has no dependency to other projects in the solution. All other projects depend on this directly or indirectly.
titd: This is the domain layer of the solution. It contains entities, aggregate roots, domain services, value types, repository interfaces and other domain objects.
bookent: A Book entity, a BookManager domain service and an IBookRepository interface are good examples to be inside the *.Domain project.
depenon: Depends on the *.Domain.Shared because it uses constants, enums and other objects defined in that project.
tpca: This project contains application service interfaces and Data Transfer Objects (DTO) of the application layer. It separates the interface & implementation of the application layer. In this way, the interface project can be shared to the clients as a contract package.
ibs: IBookAppService interface and BookCreationDto class are good examples to be inside the *.Application.Contracts project.
dot: Depends on the *.Domain.Shared because it may use constants, enums and other shared objects of this project in the application service interfaces and DTOs.
tpconta: This project contains the application service implementations of the interfaces defined in the .Application.Contracts project.
bookAppSer: BookAppService is the implementation of IBookAppService interface and good examples to be inside the *.Application project.
depoth: Depends on the *.Application.Contracts project to be able to implement the interfaces and use the DTOs. Depends on the *.Domain project to be able to use domain objects like entities, repository interfaces, etc... to perform the application logic.
EFCDepend: Depends on the *.Domain project to be able to use domain objects like entities, repository interfaces, etc... to perform the application logic.
tiinte: This is the integration project for the EF Core. It defines the DbContext and implements repository interfaces defined in the *.Domain project.
depenonthe: Depends on the *.Domain project to be able to reference to entities and repository interfaces.
tprojis: This project is available only if you are using EF Core as the database provider. If you select another database provider, its name will be different.
ticonsole:
This is a console application which simplifies to execute database migrations on development and production environments. When you run this application, it;

Creates the database if necessary.

Applies the pending database migrations.

Seeds initial data if needed.
noticethat: Notice that, this project has its own appsettings.json file. If you need to change the default database connection string, you must set it in its own appsettings.json .
seedInitialData: Seeding initial data is important at this point. ABP has a modular data seed infrastructure. Further information see data seeding documentation.
whileCreatingdata: While creating database and applying migrations seem only necessary for relational databases, this project is included even if you choose a NoSQL database provider (like MongoDB). In that case, it still seeds initial data which is necessary for the application startup.
depeonthe: Depends on the *.EntityFrameworkCore project (for EF Core) since it needs to access to the migrations. Depends on the *.Application.Contracts project to be able to access permission definitions, because initial data seeder grants all permissions for the admin role by default.
thisprojis: This project is used to define your API Controllers. Most of the time you don't need to manually define API Controllers since ABP's Auto API Controllers feature creates them automagically based on your application layer. However, in case, you need to write API controllers, this is the best place to do it.
dependsontheapp: Depends on the *.Application.Contracts project to be able to inject the application service interfaces.
thisisprojdef: This is project defines C# client proxies to use the HTTP APIs of the solution. You can share this library to 3rd-party clients, so they can easily consume your HTTP APIs in their .NET applications. For other type of applications, they can still use the APIs, either manually or using a tool in their own platform. Most of the time you don't need to manually create C# client proxies, thanks to ABP's Dynamic C# API Clients feature.
consoletestapp: *.HttpApi.Client.ConsoleTestApp project is a console application created to demonstrate the usage of the client proxies.
dependsonthe: Depends on the *.Application.Contracts project to be able to share the same application service interfaces and DTOs with the remote service.
WP: Notice that, you can delete this project & dependencies if you don't need to create C# client proxies for your APIs
thisprojcon: This project contains the user interface (UI) of the application if you are using ASP.NET Core MVC UI. It contains Razor pages, JavaScript files, CSS files, images and so on...
thisprojhas: This project has a appsettings.json file which contains the connection string and other configuration of the application.
depenonthehttp: Depends on the *.HttpApi since the UI layer needs to use APIs and application service interfaces of the solution.
ifyoucheck: If you check the source code of the *.Web.csproj file, you will see the references to the *.Application and the *.EntityFrameworkCore projects. These references are actually not needed while coding your UI layer, because UI layer doesn't depend on the EF Core or the Application layer's implementation. This startup template is pre-configured for the tiered deployment, where API layer is hosted in a separate server apart from the UI layer.
RA: However, if you don't choose the --tiered option when you create a solution, these references will be in the *.Web project to be able to host the Web, API and application layers in a single application endpoint. This gives you the ability to use the domain entities & repositories in your presentation layer. However, this is considered as a bad practice according to the DDD rules.
follare: Following are the architecture on how raaghu-react is being used as a part of UI application
seeethe: See the Getting Started document to create a new solution and run it for this template.
ctn: Click the name from the list below to see the documentation of the related startup template:
storeand: This module is used to store and edit template contents for the text templating system of the ABP framework. So, you may need to understand it to better understand the purpose of this module.
diffuse: There are different use cases of the text templating system. For example, the Account Module is using it to define templates for sending emails when it needs to send emails to users (like sending "password reset link" email). This module provides UI to easily edit these email templates.
anoverview: See the module description page for an overview of the module features.
moduleads: Text Template Management module adds the following items to the "Main" menu, under the "Administration" menu item:
texttem: Text Templates: List, view and filter text templates.
constfor: TextTemplateManagementMainMenuNames class has the constants for the menu item names.
pageiss: Text Templates page is used to view the list of templates defined in the application.
totheclick: Click to the Actions -> Edit Contents to edit content for a template. There are two types of UI to edit a template content:
selectA: Select a local Directory and run following command to download and output code using a local directory name where project is converted into zip
nowUnzip: now, unzip file using following command
runbelow: Run below code at root level of your project folder

tocreate: to create proxies, we run following command at root level
runfol: Run following command line to create a new Module, named BookStore on root folder and a Book page within it of React application
CS: Note:Creating a page is case sensitive. name of page should be in small letters

comprov: ABP Commercial provides official themes for ABP Commercial. You can use these themes in your applications.
successfullyAdded: Successfully added semi dark theme option on top navigation
addedPermission: Added permission on Side Navigation menus for Tenant and User
manyEnhancement: Many enhancements and fixes for the 7.2 version.
allowToImpersonate: Allow to impersonate an arbitrary user in the SaaS module.
succUserAdd: Successful User can add profile picture in MyAccounts
manyEnhancementFix: Many enhancements and fixes for the 1.1 version.
succAddedDark: Successfully added dark theme option on top navigation
appFullyCompa: Application fully compatible with ABP including built-in pages such as Dashboards, Tenant, User, settings, languages and much more.
fullyReplaceComp: Fully replaceable and customizable Components: Data table, File Uploader, Forms, email, etc.
adobeXD: Adobe XD UI kit design library.
diffLang: Different language selection options are available on the Login page.
changeLangFeature: Change language feature in dashboard and modules
enableNewFeat: Enabled new feature of auto service proxy generation with DTO, services and schemas with different http client support.
custIconLib: Custom icon library with more additions and enhancements.
introOfStorybook: Introduction of a storybook for user to view elements, components and check behavior in different states.
moduleIntegrate: Modules integrated in the applications are mentioned in the following section.
provideLogPage: Provides a login page with username and password.
provideRegPage: Provides a register page to create a new account.
impIdentityAuth: Implements Identity server authentication server UI and functionality.
provideLogReport: Provides the audit log reporting UI for the auditing infrastructure and allows to search, filter, and show audit log entities and entity change logs.
consistCritical: Consist critical data about each client request such as HTTP method, HTTP return status , request execution duration and the success failure respectively.
pageLikeTenant: Includes Pages like Tenant and Edition
allowManageTenant: Allows to manage tenants where a tenant is a customer of your application.
allowCreateedition: Allows to create editions where an edition can have different feature set of your application  assigned to tenants.
createMultiBlog: Create multiple blogs in a single application.
providePageMgt: Provides a page management system to manage dynamic pages.
blogSystem: A blog system to create blogs and publish posts.
tagSystem: Tag system to tag any kind of resources, like blog posts.
commSystem: Comment system to add comments feature to any kind of resource, like blog posts.
uploadAndOrgFile: It is used to upload and organize files in a hierarchical folder structure.
blobStoreSyst: This module is based on BLOB Storing system, where it can use different storage providers for storing file contents.
craeteServey: Create surveys, manage event registrations, collect email addresses for a newsletter , create a pop quiz or even get an order request.
manageRoleUser: Manages role and users in the system wherein user is allowed to have multiple roles.
managePermission: Allows permissions in role and user levels.
enableDisTwoFactor: Enable/disable two factor authentication and user lockout per user.
manageUserPass: Manage basic user profile and password.
manageClaimType: Manages claim types in the system , set claims to role and users.
settPagePass: Setting page to manage password complexity, user sign-in, account and lockout.
manageAppLang: Manages application languages and localization texts.
dynamicChangeTxt: Dynamically change all texts on the UI
addUpdateFeature: Features like add, remove, update the language
includeLangTxt: Includes a language text that translates the application’s UI to a new language or update texts for an existing language.
pageLikeApp: Includes pages like Application and Scopes
provideFeaturelikeEdit: Application Page providing features like add or edit applications
rangeOfPermission: A set of range of permissions available on applications list page
featureIntegration: Features includes integration for payment gateways

tosolcreate: To create a new solution, see how to create a new solution.
ht: Depends on the *.Application.Contracts project to be able to access permission definitions, because initial data seeder grants all permissions for the admin role by default.
mostofthet: Most of the time you don't need to manually define API Controllers since ABP's Auto API Controllers feature creates them automagically based on your application layer. However, in case, you need to write API controllers, this is the best place to do it.
mostofthe: Most of the time you don't need to manually create C# client proxies, thanks to ABP's <a href='https://docs.abp.io/en/abp/latest/API/Dynamic-CSharp-API-Clients'> Dynamic C# API Clients feature.
youcanvisitmod: You can visit Account module package list page to see list of packages related with this module.
head: raaghu React Documentation
text: raaghu-react is a set of professional modules,tools, themes built with the help of a backend support of ABP Commercial. raaghu consists of independent elements that acts as building blocks and components that are reusable and can be used in different modules accordingly.
allowhostpass: Allow host users to change the password of a user of a tenant.
supportsocial: Supports social login interactions (Twitter, Facebook, GitHub, etc.)
addedmenuside: Added permission on Side Navigation menus for Tenant and User
manyenhance: Many enhancements and fixes for the 7.2 version.
ofthechatmod: Integration of the chat module for different users.
linebased: Added a Command Line Based CRUD Page generation for different relationships such as one-to-one, one-to-many and many-to-many relationships.
manyenhancever: Many enhancements and fixes for the 7.2 version.
RdsCompDataTable: in RdsCompDataTable, we can add the neccessary data we want to show in tableData and following array of actions that you require as well as neccessary function using onActionSelection
